title: ApprovalRequest
description: Protocol-owned approval request family. Requests are hash-bound to immutable inputs, carry shared principal identity context, and keep approval triggers in their own registry with fail-closed runtime validation. The request itself is a signed object payload so trusted Go verification can treat approval intent as an explicit detached-signature contract.

schema_id.description: Stable protocol object-family identifier for approval requests.

schema_version.description: Exact protocol schema version used to validate this approval request.

approval_profile.description: Approval profile in effect for this request. MVP supports only the moderate profile.

requester.description: Shared principal identity for the actor requesting human approval.

approval_trigger_code.description: Machine-consumed trigger code explaining why human approval is being requested. Registry membership is validated fail-closed by runtime policy and manifest tooling rather than by a schema enum.

manifest_hash.description: Hash of the active signed manifest context bound to this approval request.

action_request_hash.description: Hash of the action request or stage summary whose approval outcome is being requested.

relevant_artifact_hashes.description: Hashes of artifacts whose exact bytes are part of the approval decision context. An empty array is valid when only the manifest and action request hashes are relevant.

items.description: Hash of one artifact that is part of the approval context.

details_schema_id.description: Schema identifier for the structured details payload shown to the human reviewer.

details.description: Structured details payload whose shape is defined by details_schema_id; freeform-only prompts are not sufficient.

approval_assurance_level.description: Requested approval assurance level independent of delivery channel. Unknown values fail closed.

presence_mode.description: Requested user-presence mode for this approval flow. Unknown values fail closed.

requested_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp recording when the approval request was issued.

expires_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp recording when the approval request expires; there is no implicit protocol default. Runtime validation must reject timestamps that do not fall after requested_at.

staleness_posture.description: Fail-closed staleness rule for the request. If any bound hash changes before approval is consumed, the request is invalid.

changes_if_approved.description: Clear human-facing summary of what will change if this request is approved.

signatures.description: Detached signatures attesting to the exact approval request payload contents over RFC 8785 JCS canonical bytes.

items.description: Detached signature block for the approval request payload.
